data.title: Intel surges as investment news bubbles; PC market stronger than expected
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares surged 8.8% by the close of market trading on Thursday, as the once-beleaguered U.S. chipmaker has climbed 37% since September 17.

Its share price has soared 69% year to date. In comparison, the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) has increased 26% year to date, while the S&P 500 Index is up 12% over the same time frame. 

Interestingly, most major semiconductor stocks finished the trading day down or remained mostly static. For example, TSMC (TSM) slid 1.5%, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) inched up 0.1%, Nvidia (NVDA) increased 0.3%, Broadcom (AVGO) slipped 0.9% and Qualcomm (QCOM) declined 2.2%. However, Marvell (MRVL) gained 4.6%.

Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has recently secured significant investments in his company. This includes a $2B investment by Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF), a 10% stake by the U.S. government and, most recently, GPU giant Nvidia's $5B backing.

Rumors surfaced on Wednesday that Intel was in discussions with Apple (AAPL) for a potential investment or a possible partnership. It has also been speculated that Intel and TSMC have reached a preliminary agreement to form a joint venture to operate some of Intel's foundry facilities. This report first surfaced way back in April, but was echoed again today in the Wall Street Journal. 

There has also been positive news recently on a strengthening PC market. The PC segment stands as Intel's most substantial driver of revenue. Micron Technology indicated earlier this week that the PC market is improving due to the Windows 11 upgrade and the emergence of artificial intelligence-powered PCs.

"Turning to PCs, end-of-life of Windows 10 and greater adoption of AI-enabled PCs are driving an improved PC demand outlook," said Micron CEO during Tuesday night's earnings call. "We now expect PC unit shipments to grow at a mid-single-digit percentage level in calendar 2025 versus our low single-digit percentage growth expectations previously."

data.title: La Rosa Holdings registers 283M shares for resale under $1B equity facility
data.description:
• La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) is registering up to 283.33M shares of common stock for resale by a selling stockholder.
  • The shares are issuable under an amended and restated equity purchase facility agreement signed on September 18, 2025.
  • The amended facility increases the commitment from $150M to $1B in common stock.
  • The selling stockholder may purchase shares at varying prices; the prospectus assumes a reserve price of $3/share.
  • The company will not receive proceeds from the resale of shares by the selling stockholder.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: CTO Realty Growth bolsters balance sheet with $150M term loan
data.description:
CTO Realty Growth (NYSE:CTO) closed on $150M in term loan financing, which will provide the cash to retire its $65M term loan early, the company said on Thursday.

The financing consisted of a new $125M term loan due September 2030 and a $25M upsizing of the company's existing term loan due September 2029. Proceeds were used to retire the $65M term loan due March 2026, representing the majority of CTO's 2026 maturities, and to reduce the balance of its revolving credit facility.

Both term loans bear interest at SOFR plus a spread determined by the company's leverage. At closing, CTO Realty (NYSE:CTO) applied existing SOFR swap agreements, resulting in an initial fixed interest rate for both the 2030 term loan and 2029 term loan of ~4.2%.  In March 2026, when certain applied SOFR swap agreements mature, they'll be replaced by existing forward SOFR swap agreements, with the interest rates for both adjusting to ~4.7%, based on the company's current leverage ratio.

CTO Realty (NYSE:CTO) stock edged up 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Fiserv acquires Smith Consulting Group
data.description:
Fiserv (NYSE:FI) announced on Thursday the acquisition of Smith Consulting Group, an operational consulting service used by community banks and credit unions.

Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The payments and financial services technology provider said Smith Consulting has been its long-standing partner.

data.title: Shoe Carnival reappoints W. Kerry Jackson as CFO
data.description:
• Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) has named W. Kerry Jackson as EVP and chief financial officer, effective September 28, 2025.
  • Jackson rejoined the company in June 2025 after retiring in May 2023; he previously served as CFO for 27 years. He has been with Shoe Carnival for a total of 35 years and was recently SVP of New Business Development.
  • Patrick C. Edwards, current CFO, will transition to SVP, treasurer and remain part of the finance leadership team.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Trump's interpretation of 'for cause' would 'eviscerate' Fed independence, Cook says in Supreme Court filing
data.description:
In a filing to the U.S. Supreme Court on Thursday, Federal Reserve Governor Lisa D. Cook's counsel said that President Donald J. Trump's case to remove her is unlikely to succeed on its merits and said his interpretation of "for cause" in purportedly firing her "would destroy the Federal Reserve's historic independence."

The president's interpretation of "for cause" is hardly distinguishable from "at will," the document said.  On Aug. 25, Trump declared that he was removing Cook from the Fed, effective immediately, after Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Bill Pulte alleged that Cook claimed two houses as her primary residence in 2021.

"Accepting that interpretation here would eviscerate the Federal Reserve’s longstanding independence, upend financial markets, and create a blueprint for future presidents to direct monetary policy based on their political agendas and election calendars," her counsel, Abbe Lowell said in the argument.

Other prongs of the legal argument contend that:

  • The president's "for cause" decisions are reviewable in court, contrary to Trump's assertions;
  • Her purported removal was not "for cause;"
  • That Cook was deprived of the legally required process, including an opportunity to contest the claims against her;
  • The district court's preliminary injunction that keeps her on the Fed while her suit proceeds is proper; and
  • The president won't be irreparably harmed by a stay that would keep Cook in place during the suit.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Mexico's central bank cuts interest rate by 25 basis points to 7.50%
data.description:
The board of governors of Mexico's central bank on Thursday voted to cut its benchmark overnight lending rate by 25 basis points to 7.50%, in line with market expectations. Banxico, as the bank is known, cited sluggishness in economic activity in the third quarter and significant downside risks from trade tensions for its move.

Latin America's second-biggest economy has been struggling with sticky inflation and a tumultuous tariff relationship with the United States.

Latest government data showed annual headline inflation increasing to 3.74% in the third quarter, from 3.51%. Core inflation also ticked up to 4.26% from 4.23%. Meanwhile, the International Monetary Fund projects annual real GDP growth of only 0.2% in 2025 for Mexico, down from 2024's 1.4%.       

"The Governing Board deemed appropriate to continue the rate-cutting cycle. This decision was consistent with the assessment of the current inflationary outlook. In particular, it took into account the behavior of the exchange rate, the weakness of economic activity, and the possible impact of changes in trade policies worldwide. It also considered the level of monetary restriction that has been implemented," Banxico said in a statement. 

The decision to cut rates by 25 basis points was not a unanimous one, Banxico said. Of the five members on the bank's board, Jonathan Heath voted in favor of keeping the key rate unchanged at 7.75%.  

"Looking ahead, the Board will assess further adjustments to the reference rate. It will take into account the effects of all determinants of inflation. Actions will be implemented in such a way that the reference rate remains consistent at all times with the trajectory needed to enable an orderly and sustained convergence of headline inflation to the 3% target during the forecast period," Banxico said.

"The central bank reaffirms its commitment to its primary mandate and the need to continue its efforts to consolidate an environment of low and stable inflation," it added.

Here are some Mexico-linked exchange-traded funds of interest: (NYSEARCA:EWW), FLMX), and (NYSEARCA:MEXX).

data.title: Wall Street ended in the red after jobless claims fell and Q2 GDP was revised higher
data.description:
Wall Street finished lower in a roller coaster session on Thursday as the broader market averages observed selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) had dropped 0.5%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the red by 0.5% as well and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished downward by 0.3%. 

Across sectors, nine of the 11 S&P groups concluded in negative trading territory, with Health Care suffering the most. At the other end, Energy was the strongest performing segment on the session as oil (CL1:COM) climbed 0.7%. 

U.S. Treasury yields also moved higher. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 3 basis points to 4.18% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 5 basis points to 3.66%. A 7-year note auction tailed, indicating weaker demand for government-backed fixed-income securities.

At the same time the dollar index (DXY) tagged a 3-week high.

On the economic front, initial jobless claims dropped for the second week.

Q2 GDP was revised higher to +3.8% in the third estimate, intensifying the rebound.

Durable goods orders surprised higher in August, while the U.S. international goods trade deficit narrowed more than expected in August.

"Real GDP numbers were good, jobless claims were decent, and we didn’t see any major bad news in other areas. To me, stock weakness is just the result of some profit-taking. After a few extremely strong months, the market leaders have become quite richly valued, making the market prone to days like today. I wouldn’t read too much into it,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

“With regard to tomorrow’s PCE numbers, unless we get a substantially higher number, I doubt the market will start to price in fewer Fed rate cuts,” Nelissen added.

“Thursday's upward GDP revision for second quarter confirmed that the economy grew at a healthy clip, even as tariff uncertainty reached fever pitch during the quarter. The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

“Thursday's GDP strength likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected path of rate cuts, since the data is backward looking and was already well-known. The Fed wants to lower rates to keep the labor market in check and is able to do so at this time since inflation has remained under control, even though it's still above the Fed's target,” Stanley added.

Furthermore, the Fed balance sheet will come after the close.

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, all took the stage at different events.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Intel (INTC) advanced on the session by 8.8%, while Oracle (ORCL) declined by 5.5%.
